DISCOVERY 302, PETITIONER 350 BEACH LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY'S MOTION FOR PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDAMUS. NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - CONTINUED TO 8/11/11, FOR THE MOVING PARTY TO PROVIDE COURTESY COPY OF MOVING POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE. SEE SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULE 2.6(B). JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI; NOT REPORTED

PETITIONER 350 BEACH LLC'S MOTION FOR PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDAMUS IS DENIED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE BOARD OF APPEAL DID NOT ABUSE ITS DISCRETION UNDER THE SUBSTANTIAL EVIDENCE LIST. THE BOARD RELIED ON SUBSTANTIAL EVIDENCE WHEN THEY UPHELD THE ZONING ADMINISTRATOR'S DECISION. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE LORETTA M. calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Mandamus
Off Calendar Aug-23-2011 Denied
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER 350 BEACH LLC'S MOTION FOR PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDAMUS.
Appearances: Alison B. Torbitt and Bruce E. Copeland of Nixon Peabody (984.5008) for petitioner and moving party; Diana M. Saka (no card provided) for Sabella Trust; Andrea Ruiz-Esquide (no card provided) for City and County of San Francisco.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Petitioner's motion for a writ of administrative mandamus is denied. The court finds that the Board of Appeal did not abuse its discretion under the substantial evidence list. The Board relied on substantial evidence when they upheld the Zoning Administrator's decision. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge Loretta M. Giorgi; Reporter: Loretta Najera, CSR #5153
